programme.desc: Observational documentary following the work of Northumbria Police across Newcastle, Sunderland and beyond, telling stories of the force's officers and victims of crime. Stuart and Rainie go out for dinner, but he becomes increasingly concerned about her behaviour. Adam invites Habiba out, only for Honey to suggest they spend some time together(n)

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: The dry season is now upon the animals - a tough time for some, but for others a food bonanza. Jasari and his wild dog family are under siege from the hyena clan, so he must train his pups to survive, while Bakari the baboon is living in exile from the troop, only to spy the leader bullying his adopted baby. Kali and her cubs are enjoying life back in the heart of the pride, only to be attacked by the black-maned lions. But worse is to come when a storm ignites a devastating fire, consuming the dry land with flames(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: The team examines an 18th-century portrait attributed to pioneering female artist Maria Cosway, but which Philip Mould believes may actually be an undiscovered work by the great Regency artist Sir Thomas Lawrence. He noticed it during a visit to the owners' home and now the time has come to put his hunch to the test. What's more, the difference in value is enormous - as a work by Cosway, it is worth about £8,000. If the presenter is right, it could be more in the region of half a million(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actress Naomie Harris's dramatic discoveries in this edition of the show in which celebrities research their family trees inspires her to anger, sadness and tears of joy. On a journey from Trinidad to Grenada to Jamaica, she learns of her great-great-great-great-grandfather's involvement in the exploitation of so-called liberated Africans shortly after the abolition of slavery, is delighted to discover the identity of her African five-times-great grandmother, and uncovers a family story of tragic poverty in Jamaica's capital of Kingston(n)

programme.title: An Island Parish
programme.desc: It's Christmas and the islanders are singing carols and preparing their traditional Falklands roast lamb, but the Rev Richard Hines and his wife Jen have travelled 800 miles to celebrate the big day in the furthest corner of his parish, the tiny island of South Georgia. Back in Stanley, the elves are wrapping presents for Flying Santa to deliver to the children on the least accessible farms and settlements, while the owners of the fastest horses are converging on the racecourse ready for the annual Boxing Day meeting, where veteran jockey Ron Binnie is making his 50th appearance, alongside his grandson Duane(n)

programme.title: An Island Parish

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.sub-title: Dame Kelly: The Power of Parkrun - Our Lives
programme.desc: Parkrun started out in London as a simple idea to get friends together for a time trial and a coffee, but now attracts more than 235,000 participants around the globe every weekend. In this film, double Olympic gold medallist Kelly Holmes explores the popularity of the events and the mental health benefits of running in the build-up to a new Parkrun in Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: More Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Documentary following the lives and work of rural vets. In the first edition, farm vet Richard helps a sheep who has suddenly gone blind in Teesdale, and a spaniel proves a handful for small animal vet Steve. Plus, Adam deals with the case of a homeless kitten run over by a car. Narrated by Felicity Kendal(n)

programme.title: Elephant Family & Me
programme.desc: The second of two programmes in which Gordon Buchanan encounters a family of African elephants in the wilderness of Kenya, and attempts to gain their trust so he can observe them up close in their natural habitat(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring three treasured family heirlooms - and the memories they hold - back to life, tackling a 1960s cine-projector, a wicker chair and a hand-crafted spinning wheel that has travelled all the way from Shetland(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a cherry Bakewell factory in Stoke on Trent that produces 250,000 tarts every day, and meets the team of 12 employed to precisely place the cherries on top by hand. Cherry Healey learns the tricks of avoiding a soggy pastry bottom when baking at home, and visits a factory where almonds are roasted and milled to make butter. Historian Ruth Goodman discovers the origins of frangipane as a perfume in 17th-century Paris, and discovers how the modern cherry Bakewell recipe was arrived at by accident(n)

programme.title: The Chefs' Brigade
programme.desc: Jason Atherton takes 10 chefs from pubs, cafes and caterers across the UK on a culinary journey across Europe, and gives them a crash course in preparing food fit for the finest restaurants. The first destination is Puglia in southern Italy, where food is prepared on the principle of making the most of what is to hand. The chefs are tested on their pasta-making skills, and pitted in a culinary contest against a well-regarded local restaurant. However, Jason faces a tough choice of whether to send a struggling member of the group home and fly in a replacement(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain hosts as regular panellist Hugh Dennis looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of fellow comedians Tom Allen, Ed Gamble, Kerry Godliman, Rhys James and Sindhu Vee. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another(n)

programme.title: Newsnight

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: Romesh heads to more countries that are rarely visited by tourists to get a sense of the local culture and find out if they deserve their reputation. In this first edition, he heads to Zimbabwe in the aftermath of Robert Mugabe's rule, to discover whether British people should be holidaying in the southern African country once again. He meets Chipo, a teacher who was born and raised in Harare under Mugabe's rule. She explains that although people have been coming to the country, few venture beyond Victoria Falls. She wants to show Rom what they are missing on a grand, epic road trip that takes in National Parks and close-up encounters with rhinos, elephants and the so-called 'cheetah ambassador'(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.sub-title: Inside the Social Network: Facebook's Difficult Year
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes at Facebook, the world's biggest social media company, with nearly a third of the planet's population connected to its networks. However, the company has suffered a series of deepening scandals and intense media scrutiny, with data leaks, fake news and security hacks all threatening to destroy everything Mark Zuckerberg had built. Throughout this difficult time, Facebook allowed the BBC to follow key members of the team trying to fix the problems and secure the platform(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein revisit their investigation into the historic case, conviction and execution of Alfred Moore, who murdered two policemen(n)

programme.title: Fugitives
programme.desc: Following the investigation by an elite team of Scotland Yard detectives into the biggest organised crime gangs in London, which uncovered the drugs kingpin behind a multi-million-pound racket. By making his own drugs delivery, Paul Monk blew his cover and, when his associates were arrested, fled abroad. Plus, the Irish Garda search for a killer who went to extreme lengths to dispose of his victim's body(n)

programme.title: An Island Parish
programme.desc: It's high summer in the Falkland Islands, and as the Rev Richard Hines takes over as guest presenter on local radio - if technological gremlins will allow him - his wife Jen braves stiff competition from the resident bakers as she enters her home-made produce in the annual show. The islanders also welcome some honoured guests, as veterans of the 1982 conflict return to remember their fallen comrades who perished when the Sir Galahad was bombed in Port Pleasant(n)

programme.title: An Island Parish
programme.desc: As summer draws to a close, vet Steve Pointing inspects the foreign vessels arriving in Stanley to get their permits to go fishing for South Atlantic squid. Back at his surgery, he treats a gentoo penguin that has been mauled by a sea lion. The Rev Richard Hines and his wife Jen indulge in the popular Falklands pastime of diddle-dee berry picking to make jam, and the islanders bid a fond farewell to governor Nigel Haywood, who is heading off to his retirement(n)

programme.title: Gorilla Family and Me
programme.desc: Part one of two. Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan travels to the Democratic Republic of the Congo, where he realises a lifelong dream by spending time with Grauer's gorillas, the largest primates on earth. He meets the fearsome alpha male Chimanuka and two babies, one a lonely orphan and as Gordon gains the trust of these mysterious, seldom seen animals, he discovers their incredible compassion and gets caught up in a battle between giant rival silverbacks for control of the family(n)

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: Leading chefs take maitre d' and First Dates star Fred Sirieix to experience dining at their favourite restaurants and explore what goes into making them so special. This time, chef Michel Roux Jr takes Fred Sirieix to some of his favourite restaurants in Paris. First, the pair visit one of the most famous restaurants in the city, La Tour D'Argent, where they experience a signature dish that has been on the menu since 1890. Moving away from restaurants that offer expensive indulgence, Michel takes Fred to a place reviving one of the simplest and cheapest French classics - the ham baguette. For the final stop of their Parisian trip, the pair visit Brasserie La Couple, which boasts the biggest dining room in Paris(n)

programme.title: Animal Babies: First Year on Earth
programme.desc: Cameras follow six baby animals as they learn the basic skills needed to face the challenges of surviving their first year on Earth. In California, a three-month old sea otter pup has to learn what's safe to eat in a world enmeshed with humans, while in Sri Lanka, a seven-month old macaque is forced by his mother to toughen up. In Kenya, a five-month old elephant bond with new families(n)

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam almost immediately hits it off with Robin at her friend's haiku reading event, and the next day he calls with an offer to take her on a weekend away. Comedy-drama, starring Pamela Adlon, Olivia Edward, and Hannah Alligood(n)

credits.actor: Pamela Adlon, Olivia Edward,, Hannah Alligood

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef heads to Berlin, where history and the avant garde dwell side by side. A city that once made history with its divisive wall and Checkpoint Charlie now teems with an overwhelming array of innovative chefs offering delicious seasonal creations, such as pureed sunflower seeds, Jerusalem artichoke flans and crushed frozen pine nuts. Loved by the likes of Greta Garbo, Charlie Chaplin and Auguste Escoffier there's still a cornucopia of choice for those with a more traditional temperament with eisbein, meatballs and sausages topping every menu in the German capital(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: The Chefs' Brigade
programme.desc: Jason Atherton takes 10 chefs from pubs, cafes and caterers across the UK on a culinary journey across Europe, and gives them a crash course in preparing food fit for the finest restaurants. The first destination is Puglia in southern Italy, where food is prepared on the principle of making the most of what is to hand. The chefs are tested on their pasta-making skills, and pitted in a culinary contest against a well-regarded local restaurant. However, Jason faces a tough choice of whether to send a struggling member of the group home and fly in a replacement(n)

programme.title: Remarkable Places to Eat
programme.desc: Leading chefs take maitre d' and First Dates star Fred Sirieix to experience dining at their favourite restaurants and explore what goes into making them so special. In the first edition, Fred heads to Venice in the company of chef-restaurateur Angela Hartnett whose Italian family heritage has given her a lifelong love of Italian food. She takes him enjoy the simple but stunning food at her favourite restaurant Osteria Alle Testiere and the unforgettable service at Caffè Florian - one of the oldest cafes in Europe(n)

programme.title: Imagine
programme.sub-title: Imagine - Edna O'Brien: Fearful and Fearless
programme.desc: Alan Yentob meets Irish novelist Edna O'Brien and discusses her literary career, from her controversial 1960 debut The Country Girls, which was banned in Ireland, to her most recent work, Girl, inspired by the schoolgirls abducted by Boko Haram. She discusses sex, books and a lifetime of striving to break down social and sexual barriers for women(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Secret History of the British Garden
programme.desc: Monty Don examines how British gardens have changed over the past 400 years. The first episode focuses on the 17th century, and features a visit to Levens Hall in Cumbria, the only garden from the 1600s that survives to the present day. He also examines archaeological evidence of a lost garden and uncovers hidden message and religious meaning in an aerial photograph taken during the Second World War. Finally, he pays a visit to Hampton Court to see the influence of the Civil War on garden design(n)

programme.title: An Island Parish
programme.desc: Documentary following life in the British overseas territory in the south Atlantic, where it is autumn and the Rev Richard Hines is preparing for Palm Sunday, which involves collecting enough tussock grass to make 100 crosses by hand. Over on Saunders Island, the Pole-Evans family are preparing to send the sheep off to market, while the islanders are ready to celebrate the Queen's birthday with a parade and reception at Government House(n)

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin meets the gardeners of Evington in Leicester hoping to win their first gold, including elderly volunteers who have revived an overgrown area of the local park and members of a gardening club who have transformed a strip of land at a primary school into a bee-friendly wildlife area(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo picks up his rail journey from Warwick to Rye. In Reading, he traces the origins of education for workers, and in Cliveden explores a great Italianate mansion(n)

programme.title: BBC News at 9
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Anita Rani and Martin Hughes-Games join team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke on the panel show that tests wildlife knowledge, with Kate Humble asking the questions(n)

programme.title: Getting the Builders In
programme.desc: Three pairs of builders compete with one another to win the job of transforming a toy-stuffed playroom in Oxted, Surrey, into a teenage den on a budget of £5,000(n)

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: Actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of baking recipes from TV chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explain how necessity often forced cooks to make the most of limited ingredients to create tasty dishes. They reveal that many popular meals were invented during hard times, from mince and dumplings to Scotch broths(n)

programme.title: More Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: A critically ill miniature donkey foal makes an emotional emergency case for equine vet Kerry, and Richard creates an operating table from a forklift truck. At the Small Animal Hospital, senior vet Adam's job is anything but routine when he X-rays a tortoise and treats a gecko with a problem eye. Narrated by Felicity Kendal(n)

programme.title: Gorilla Family and Me
programme.desc: Part two of two. Gordon Buchanan returns to the Democratic Republic of Congo in the hope of finding the gorilla family again, and discovering the outcome of silverback Chimanuka's confrontation with a rival. He soon finds the apes, but discovers their lives are now under threat from poachers, and works with the local rangers to prevent them coming to harm(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Paul Laidlaw visit Birmingham, where one grisly find features a gallows and a break from the shops uncovers the story of the real Peaky Blinders(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Singers and performers Bobby Crush, Nicholas McCarthy, Sonia, Sandi Bogle and Sam Bailey take on the general-knowledge champions. Hosted by Jeremy Vine(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: British entrepreneurs' products are put to the test, with consumers unboxing, trying out and giving their feedback on each invention(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore more family heirlooms, including a tired and dilapidated a rocking horse that holds huge sentimental significance for its owner and a 1930s pinball machine. Other items are a Victorian device used for measuring weather pressure and a pair of precious plates(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Documentary series following the ups and downs of six farming families in Scotland, from Argyll to the Outer Hebrides, as they face up to tough economic times and an uncertain future. Crofting couple Lynn and Sandra face the danger of a drought, while buffalo farmer Stevie's herd go for a cooling dip in the loch. At the Royal Highland Show, Raymond and Johnny are hoping for success with their Charolais cattle, while Aimee puts her pony skills to the test(n)

programme.title: Broke
programme.desc: Documentary following more people who are in work but struggling to make ends meet. They include Tyrone, a homeless teenager who works a zero-hour contract at McDonald's and wants to rent his own flat - but cannot guarantee he will earn enough each week to cover the rent. Plus, the story of Ecuadorian immigrant Angelica, who lives in London, where she studies English all day and works night shifts as a cleaner. After requesting a pay rise to the living wage, she was suspended by her employment agency(n)

programme.title: Vic & Bob's Big Night Out
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer showcases his incredible new dance routine and eventually forces a reluctant Vic Reeves to join in. Plus, characters Donald and Davey Stott take to the stage to perform an ambitious magic routine, and The Man With The Stick makes a long awaited return, before Vic and Bob end the show with a performance of their song, You Can Do It(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emma Barnett(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Golf: Women's British Open
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from day one at Woburn Golf Club, near Milton Keynes, where England's Georgia Hall entered as the defending champion(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Venice 24/7
programme.desc: The work of emergency services during the city's annual carnival, which celebrates decadence in the run-up to Lent. Undercover police officers try to catch thieves, while their colleagues investigate a dead body found near St Mark's Square. Elsewhere, medics treat a tourist who is believed to have overdosed on drugs(n)

programme.title: SAS: Rogue Warriors
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre uses the SAS archives to examine the history of the British Army special forces unit combining documents, unseen footage and interviews with its founding members to tell the remarkable story behind an extraordinary fighting force. In the first edition, he looks at its formation in the heat of the north African desert in 1941, when eccentric young British army officer David Archibald Stirling had a vision for a new kind of war in which the enemy was attacked from behind their own lines(n)

programme.title: Revolutions: The Ideas That Changed the World
programme.desc: Jim Al-Khalili traces the history behind the invention of the car. The journey begins with the earliest known evidence of dog sleds, dating back 9,000 years to a settlement in Siberia and pulled by domesticated grey wolves. The programme moves on to the invention of the wheel - and the bronze tools that made its creation possible - and the tragic story of Carl Benz, who invented a motor car but failed to sell any. Finally, there is a look at modern solutions to the pollution caused by motor vehicles(n)

programme.title: Art, Passion & Power: The Story of the Royal Collection
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon explores how royal collecting has changed since the days of Queen Victoria. He also reveals how women, both queens and consorts, made their mark on the Collection, and used art to steady the ship of monarchy through an uncertain age(n)

programme.title: The Extraordinary Collector
programme.desc: Gordon Watson tries to sell an item to Anita Zabludowicz, the world-renowned billionaire collector of unusual and experimental contemporary art. Anita has two rooms named after her at the Tate Modern, and a large art gallery in London as well as other spaces in Finland and New York. Her and Gordon have known one another for 20 years, but in all that time he's never managed to sell anything to her - can he end that run on this occasion?(n)

programme.title: The Extraordinary Collector
programme.desc: Gordon Watson's business dovetails with that of the interior designers furnishing the homes of London's super rich, and over the years he has become the go-to man when it comes to buying appropriate furniture, art and other objects to give that extra special touch. In the last progeramme of the series, cameras follow Gordon to Pad, an annual design fair in central London, where he hopes his hand-picked pieces will tempt the uber rich and their clients visiting his stand to part with their cash(n)

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Edmund receives a visit from the bloodthirsty Bishop of Bath and Wells, who threatens to do him a mischief with a red-hot poker over the matter of an unpaid loan. As he attempts to raise the £1,000 he needs, he finds himself being constantly summoned to the court by the Queen. Comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson and Miranda Richardson(n)

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Lord Melchett challenges Edmund to a drinking contest that he cannot get out of - but his puritanical uncle and aunt Lord and Lady Whiteadder are visiting on the same night, leaving him trying to be in two places at once. Comedy, guest starring Miriam Margolyes, with Rowan Atkinson and Stephen Fry(n)

credits.actor: Miriam Margolyes, Rowan Atkinson, Stephen Fry

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Edmund and Lord Melchett are embarrassingly kidnapped by a maniacal German master of disguise with designs on the English throne. Unfortunately for them, the Queen decides to pay only one of the ransom demands. Comedy, guest starring Hugh Laurie, with Rowan Atkinson and Stephen Fry(n)

credits.actor: Hugh Laurie, Rowan Atkinson, Stephen Fry

programme.title: SAS: Rogue Warriors
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre uses the SAS archives to examine the history of the British Army special forces unit combining documents, unseen footage and interviews with its founding members to tell the remarkable story behind an extraordinary fighting force. In the first edition, he looks at its formation in the heat of the north African desert in 1941, when eccentric young British army officer David Archibald Stirling had a vision for a new kind of war in which the enemy was attacked from behind their own lines(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Venice 24/7
programme.desc: Officials prepare anxiously for the city's first papal visit in a quarter of a century, and work carefully to ensure the already uneasy relationship between Venice and the Vatican is not aggravated further. Security is tightened across the city, specially selected papal gondoliers are prepared, and frogmen survey the waterways for potential hazards - but police attempts to close the Grand Canal upset some residents(n)

programme.title: This World
programme.sub-title: The Tea Trail with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the first of two This World documentaries examining the stories behind the nation's favourite beverages, adventurer and broadcaster Simon Reeve looks at the production of tea. He begins in the Kenyan coastal city of Mombasa, where most of the leaves for teabags are bought at auction, before travelling through eastern Africa to meet some of the millions of people who pick, pack and transport it. He drinks tea with everyone from Maasai cattle herders to the descendants of the original plantation owners, and hears claims of low wages and child labour(n)

programme.title: The First Georgians: The German Kings Who Made Britain
programme.desc: Dr Lucy Worsley reveals the history of Britain under the reigns of George I and George II, in a programme originally shown to mark the 300th anniversary of the Hanoverian succession to the British throne. She is given access to treasure from the Royal Collection at the Queen's Gallery, Buckingham Palace, to give a personal insight into the monarchs' lives, and begins by charting the story of George I's feuding family(n)

programme.title: Mary Beard's Ultimate Rome: Empire Without Limit
programme.desc: English Classical scholar Mary Beard explores the physical world of the Roman Empire, finding surprising parallels with our own world. She discovers a vast empire bound together by a common material culture, and a globalised economy of such scale that evidence of its side-effects can still be seen today. As Mary examines a huge commercial and cultural network, she takes in the vital supply of olive oil to Rome, the slave trade, and the silver mines of Spain. Following the famous Roman road network, the presenter also reveals another side to the Roman Empire, where builders and traders eclipse soldiers, and slaves make the most of a hugely connected new world(n)

programme.title: Size Matters
programme.desc: Hannah Fry presents the first of two documentaries in which she looks at the science of size. She begins by imagining what life on Earth would be like if the planet were much bigger than it already is, learning about the biggest ape to ever exist and imagining what a 15m tall human body would look like(n)

programme.title: Tomorrow's Worlds: The Unearthly History of Science Fiction
programme.desc: Dominic Sandbrook continues his exploration of the genre by looking at its obsession with robots. The idea of playing God and creating artificial life has fascinated writers since the earliest days of sci-fi. Among the creations he examines are Frankenstein's monster, Doctor Who's Cybermen and the time-travelling cyborg of The Terminator, as well as considering the worlds of Blade Runner, Battlestar Galactica and The Matrix. Featuring contributions by actors Rutger Hauer (Blade Runner), Peter Weller (RoboCop), Edward James Olmos (Battlestar Galactica) and Anthony Daniels and Kenny Baker (C3PO and R2D2 from Star Wars), as well as producer Gale Anne Hurd (The Terminator) and cyberpunk novelist William Gibson(n)

programme.title: The Many Primes of Muriel Spark
programme.desc: Kirsty Wark presents a celebration of the author of The Prime of Miss Jean Brodie on the 100th anniversary of her birth. She revisits Spark's Edinburgh roots, talks to biographer Alan Taylor and conducts the first televised interview with Penelope Jardine, who was a close friend of the writer for 40 years. With contributions from AL Kennedy, Janice Galloway, Ali Smith, William Boyd and Val McDermid(n)

programme.title: This World
programme.sub-title: The Tea Trail with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: In the first of two This World documentaries examining the stories behind the nation's favourite beverages, adventurer and broadcaster Simon Reeve looks at the production of tea. programme.title: Beyond 100 Days

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Steve Wright and Bruno Brookes introduce the edition first aired on April 14, featuring Bananarama, Natalie Cole, Climie Fisher, Def Leppard, S'Express, T'Pau, Jermaine Stewart, Jellybean featuring Adele Bertei, Pebbles, Pet Shop Boys and Bros(n)

programme.title: Skies Above Britain
programme.desc: The complex and hidden world of the skies above us and the human stories of the people who spend their life in flight, beginning with how the skies are safeguarded. Air traffic controllers deal with an unidentified aircraft flying across Gatwick's flight path and the RAF scramble a typhoon jet to intercept an unresponsive plane. Elsewhere, two vintage plane enthusiasts explore their continued passion for flight, despite having faced personal tragedy in the air(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of Rockpools
programme.desc: Palaeontologist Professor Richard Fortey explores the species that live within the seaside water formations, some of which have existed for millions of years. He is joined by some of the UK's leading marine biologists at the National Marine Aquarium in Plymouth to reveal new insights into the creatures' lives(n)

programme.title: Woolly Mammoth: Secrets from the Ice
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores the natural history of the Ice Age mammals that once roamed across what is now Europe and Asia. Detailed information about their lives has been gleaned from carcasses preserved in the Siberian permafrost, and the anatomist meets scientists using genetic, chemical and molecular tests to reveal the adaptations that allowed mammoths to evolve from tropical animals to creatures capable of surviving extreme cold(n)

programme.title: Size Matters
programme.desc: Hannah Fry imagines a world in which things are smaller. She finds out what would happen if the Earth was half the size it is now, shrinks people to just 5mm tall and finds out the minimum size of the sun and other stars(n)

Bakari challenges the jealous leader of the baboon troop, leading to a violent battle, and elephant mother Nalla has to decide the future of her badly behaved teenager Tembo. Shani the zebra mother has no choice but to cross the river, fully aware a crocodile is waiting - and if he does not get her, the currents might well sweep her away(n)

programme.desc: Derek Brockway walks from Abersoch to Pwllheli on the Llyn Peninsula, finding some calming beach yoga a good antidote to a testing cliff climb. programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: With Phil left for dead, those involved take drastic action to cover their tracks - but how far are they willing to go to keep their name out of the frame? Bernadette grows frustrated as Karen and Caren continue their date and forces her mum to think about what she is doing. Stuart and Rainie go out for dinner, but he becomes increasingly concerned about her behaviour. Adam invites Habiba out, only for Honey to suggest they spend some time together(n)

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: The dry season is now upon the animals - a tough time for some, but for others a food bonanza. Jasari and his wild dog family are under siege from the hyena clan, so he must train his pups to survive, while Bakari the baboon is living in exile from the troop, only to spy the leader bullying his adopted baby. Kali and her cubs are enjoying life back in the heart of the pride, only to be attacked by the black-maned lions. But worse is to come when a storm ignites a devastating fire, consuming the dry land with flames(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: The team examines an 18th-century portrait attributed to pioneering female artist Maria Cosway, but which Philip Mould believes may actually be an undiscovered work by the great Regency artist Sir Thomas Lawrence. He noticed it during a visit to the owners' home and now the time has come to put his hunch to the test. What's more, the difference in value is enormous - as a work by Cosway, it is worth about £8,000. If the presenter is right, it could be more in the region of half a million(n)

programme.title: Saving Britain's Worst Zoo
programme.desc: The documentary follows novice zookeepers Tracey and Dean Tweedy's attempts to recapture their escaped lynx, a story that hit the international headlines. With the cat classed as a dangerous wild animal, the zoo was forced to close down for public safety. However, despite many sightings and close encounters, all attempts to capture the animal failed, and when six sheep were found in a farmer's field, the council called in a marksman(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Local hardman Phil spends the day throwing his weight around, making a fool of Keanu for getting involved in his dodgy dealings, and then having a nasty run-in with Kat. programme.title: Keeping Faith
programme.desc: The lawyer steps in to represent Madlen Vaughan after she fires her entire legal team. While the decision wins the support of Cerys, it also sets them both on a collision course with Tom. Steve wrestles with his personal feelings concerning Faith, Evan discovers he has a mysterious benefactor in prison, and DI Breeze turns his attentions towards Faith's troubling association with Gael Reardon. Eve Myles, Bradley Freegard and Aimee-Ffion Edwards star in the second series of the Wales-set legal drama(n)

programme.desc: Documentary following the investigators tracking down crooks who steal £1.25billion every year from the NHS - from organised crime rings to NHS staff themselves. programme.title: Our Cops in the North
programme.desc: Observational documentary following the work of Northumbria Police across Newcastle, Sunderland and beyond, telling stories of the force's officers and victims of crime. With top-to-bottom access to the force, the series follows crimes from murder to motorbike theft and poaching to pub fights, as call handlers, urgent responders, detectives and commanders work around the clock to protect the public and solve their cases. It goes beyond the adrenaline-fuelled blue lights, to give profound insight into the real experience of those fighting crime and those who become victims of it(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actress Naomie Harris's dramatic discoveries in this edition of the show in which celebrities research their family trees inspires her to anger, sadness and tears of joy. On a journey from Trinidad to Grenada to Jamaica, she learns of her great-great-great-great-grandfather's involvement in the exploitation of so-called liberated Africans shortly after the abolition of slavery, is delighted to discover the identity of her African five-times-great grandmother, and uncovers a family story of tragic poverty in Jamaica's capital of Kingston(n)

programme.desc: Documentary following the investigators tracking down crooks who steal £1.25billion every year from the NHS - from organised crime rings to NHS staff themselves. Investigators track down a GP practice manager who awarded himself a pay rise and a worker at a cancer hospital sends NHS money into personal accounts(n)

programme.title: Shop Well for Less?
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern help the Mountford family from Selby in Yorkshire change the way they shop in an attempt to cut down the family's spending. To shock the family into making some big changes, Steph and Alex build a surprise pop-up shop in the family's house with some of their biggest spends, but how will they react when they know the true cost of their big brand spending?(n)

programme.title: Our Cops in the North
programme.desc: Observational documentary following the work of Northumbria Police across Newcastle, Sunderland and beyond, telling stories of the force's officers and victims of crime. With top-to-bottom access to the force, the series follows crimes from murder to motorbike theft and poaching to pub fights, as call handlers, urgent responders, detectives and commanders work around the clock to protect the public and solve their cases. It goes beyond the adrenaline-fuelled blue lights, to give profound insight into the real experience of those fighting crime and those who become victims of it(n)

programme.title: My First Pride
programme.desc: The story of Deline and her daughter Émíne, who share contrasting feelings surrounding Émíne's sexuality, as they prepare to attend Belfast Pride festival. Émíne came out over Facebook and has found immediate acceptance with her friends at school, but Deline is nervous about what could happen when her daughter attends the festival. Will the reality of the event change Deline's mind?(n)

programme.title: Inside the Secret World of Incels
programme.desc: Documentary offering a never-before-seen look at the incel community, an online subculture to which multiple mass murders and hate crimes against women have been attributed(n)

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: The great migration leads to a series of confrontations for the families on the great savannah. Tragedy strikes for lioness Kali, forcing her to make a difficult decision. Bakari challenges the jealous leader of the baboon troop, leading to a violent battle, and elephant mother Nalla has to decide the future of her badly behaved teenager Tembo. Shani the zebra mother has no choice but to cross the river, fully aware a crocodile is waiting - and if he does not get her, the currents might well sweep her away(n)

Bernadette grows frustrated as Karen and Caren continue their date and forces her mum to think about what she is doing. Stuart and Rainie go out for dinner, but he becomes increasingly concerned about her behaviour. Adam invites Habiba out, only for Honey to suggest they spend some time together(n)

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: The dry season is now upon the animals - a tough time for some, but for others a food bonanza. Jasari and his wild dog family are under siege from the hyena clan, so he must train his pups to survive, while Bakari the baboon is living in exile from the troop, only to spy the leader bullying his adopted baby. Kali and her cubs are enjoying life back in the heart of the pride, only to be attacked by the black-maned lions. But worse is to come when a storm ignites a devastating fire, consuming the dry land with flames(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: The team examines an 18th-century portrait attributed to pioneering female artist Maria Cosway, but which Philip Mould believes may actually be an undiscovered work by the great Regency artist Sir Thomas Lawrence. He noticed it during a visit to the owners' home and now the time has come to put his hunch to the test. What's more, the difference in value is enormous - as a work by Cosway, it is worth about £8,000. If the presenter is right, it could be more in the region of half a million(n)

programme.title: Turas a' Bhradain (The Salmon's Journey)
programme.desc: Neen Mackay visits the annual Salmon Festival in Inverness. She meets with female anglers who are fast becoming a major part of the world of fly fishing, and with passionate competitors in casting. With the salmon species now under serious threat, the programme also considers some of the scientific debates currently going on within the industry(n)

programme.title: Bha mi Latha Samhriadh (When Stornoway Was Dancing)
programme.desc: A warm-hearted look back at aspects of the social life of Stornoway following the Second World War. It was a time of enormous change, with increasing wealth and a new culture imposing itself through music and film, but for a few halcyon years the baby-boomers uniquely enjoyed the best of both worlds(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: Counting Tigers: A Survival Special
programme.desc: Documentary examining a wildlife survey being conducted to take stock of India's tiger population, the results of which could be monumentally important to the animal's future. The camera crew is given unprecedented access to follow the survey across 50 tiger reserves and 400,000km in search of one of the most elusive predators on Earth. Narrated by Joanna Lumley(n)

programme.title: Ross Kemp Living With
programme.desc: Since leaving EastEnders and the role of hardman Grant Mitchell behind, actor Ross Kemp has turned his hand to investigative documentary making, travelling the world to lift the lid on all manner of subjects. In this four-part ITV series he stays closer to home, exploring issues facing Britain, beginning with homelessness. He joins some of Cardiff's rough sleepers on the coldest night of the year, meets families torn apart by homelessness and uncovers how much the Government actually understands about the size of the rough sleeper population(n)

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Murtaugh meets his match in former international CIA agent Wesley Cole (Seann William Scott), who returns home and assumes duty as an LAPD officer. Cole soon finds himself navigating a new partnership with Murtaugh as he adjusts to life in Los Angeles, while Avery continues his campaign for city council. With Damon Wayans, Keesha Sharp and Kevin Rahm(n)

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef ends his excursion at the Bois de Boulogne, a park on the outskirts of Paris, coinciding with the hosting of a prestigious steeplechase event at its Auteuil Hippodrome. James meets fellow Yorkshireman and jockey James Revelly and rustles up leeks vinaigrette with salmon and lamb cutlets with a colourful ratatouille. Later, the chef pays a visit to the Michelin-starred Pre Catelan restaurant, where he enjoys his favourite meal of the trip(n)

programme.title: Who's Doing the Dishes?
programme.desc: Weather presenter Sian Lloyd hosts a dinner party for four guests who win a cash prize if they can guess her identity from cryptic culinary clues - and as an added incentive, if they're successful Sian has to do the washing up. Presented by Brian McFadden(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: After a mystery sea monster washes up on a UK beach, extreme angler Jeremy Wade sets off on a journey to unmask this beast and find out whether it poses any threat to humans. His journey takes him to London, where he digs for clues among celebrated naturalist Charles Darwin's historic specimens, to the frozen waters of Greenland, where he ice fishes deeper than he's ever fished before, and on to the fjords of Norway, where he attempts to reel in one of these sea monsters before an Arctic storm rolls in(n)

programme.desc: Robert realises Tyler's awards ceremony is the same function that Michelle and Alya are catering for, so he strives to keep the two women in his life apart. Jenny and Kate have conflicting plans for Johnny's cash, and Seb tells Rachel he knows her game and he's watching her(n)

programme.title: The Best Little Prison in Britain?
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the only prison on the Isle of Man, which houses 150 inmates and is governed an entirely different legal system from the rest of the UK. The first episode introduces the man who runs the prison shop, which inmates can use depending on their behaviour, and a couple who have both been locked up - one of whom is preparing to defend himself in his retrial. Elsewhere, cameras follow Ben, an inmate who has missed his grandmother's funeral whilst being in jail, as he receives a special visit by a member of staff who have agreed to bring her ashes to him(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ray offers a stunned Michelle the role of Events Manager across his whole chain, which plays right into Robert's hands. When Abi calls at the garage and offers to help, Kevin and Tyrone enjoy winding her up, aware she's been fired from her new job. Elsewhere, Kate decides to return to work(n)

programme.title: The Widower
programme.desc: Malcolm's financial problems begin to mount, leaving him under pressure and running out of ideas. He decides he would be better off with Felicity dead and plans another fatal car crash so he and their son can start afresh, funded by her life insurance policy. Felicity's suspicious father Brian and sister Jane force Malcolm's briefcase open, and unearth his grisly plot - but must act quickly to prevent it being executed. Fact-based drama, starring Reece Shearsmith, Kate Fleetwood, James Laurenson and John Hannah(n)

programme.title: Sewer Men
programme.desc: The sewer workers at West Midlands water company Severn Trent have an unenviable task on their hands. This two-part documentary follows them as they unblock thousands of toilets and drains and delve down sewers clogged with all sorts of waste. It's a dirty job but someone has to do it - and as it turns out, these sewage shovellers just love their work. Along the way, they lift the lid on Birmingham's toilet habits, while Severn Trent's chief executive goes into emergency mode to keep the taps flowing and sort out water leaks during the driest summer in decades(n)

programme.title: The Great Fire
programme.desc: Thomas bursts into his sister-in-law's lodgings to wake his family and lead them to safety, but Sarah is nowhere to be seen. Held in a private cell at Newgate Prison, she's waiting to be interrogated by Lord Denton about a possible Catholic plot against the king. Meanwhile, Samuel brings news of the rapidly spreading blaze to Charles, who surveys the damage from the safety of the river. From this vantage point, the monarch is shocked into action, ordering his advisers to compensate the public and tear down buildings to create firebreaks, but Hyde and Ashley-Cooper have other ideas(n)

programme.title: Ross Kemp Living With
programme.desc: Since leaving EastEnders and the role of hardman Grant Mitchell behind, actor Ross Kemp has turned his hand to investigative documentary making, travelling the world to lift the lid on all manner of subjects. In this four-part ITV series he stays closer to home, exploring issues facing Britain, beginning with homelessness. He joins some of Cardiff's rough sleepers on the coldest night of the year, meets families torn apart by homelessness and uncovers how much the Government actually understands about the size of the rough sleeper population(n)

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Murtaugh meets his match in former international CIA agent Wesley Cole (Seann William Scott), who returns home and assumes duty as an LAPD officer. Cole soon finds himself navigating a new partnership with Murtaugh as he adjusts to life in Los Angeles, while Avery continues his campaign for city council. With Damon Wayans, Keesha Sharp and Kevin Rahm(n)

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef ends his excursion at the Bois de Boulogne, a park on the outskirts of Paris, coinciding with the hosting of a prestigious steeplechase event at its Auteuil Hippodrome. James meets fellow Yorkshireman and jockey James Revelly and rustles up leeks vinaigrette with salmon and lamb cutlets with a colourful ratatouille. Later, the chef pays a visit to the Michelin-starred Pre Catelan restaurant, where he enjoys his favourite meal of the trip(n)

programme.title: Who's Doing the Dishes?

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: After a mystery sea monster washes up on a UK beach, extreme angler Jeremy Wade sets off on a journey to unmask this beast and find out whether it poses any threat to humans. His journey takes him to London, where he digs for clues among celebrated naturalist Charles Darwin's historic specimens, to the frozen waters of Greenland, where he ice fishes deeper than he's ever fished before, and on to the fjords of Norway, where he attempts to reel in one of these sea monsters before an Arctic storm rolls in(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: After a mystery sea monster washes up on a UK beach, extreme angler Jeremy Wade sets off on a journey to unmask this beast and find out whether it poses any threat to humans. His journey takes him to London, where he digs for clues among celebrated naturalist Charles Darwin's historic specimens, to the frozen waters of Greenland, where he ice fishes deeper than he's ever fished before, and on to the fjords of Norway, where he attempts to reel in one of these sea monsters before an Arctic storm rolls in(n)

programme.desc: Bayern Munich v Fenerbahce (Kick-off 7.30pm). Celina Hinchcliffe presents coverage of the semi-final in the pre-season tournament, held at Allianz Arena in Munich. The hosts pipped Borussia Dortmund to the Bundesliga in May and will expect to prevail this evening against the Turkish outfit. programme.title: Million Pound Handjobs

The programme uncovers what it takes to keep model's nails in tip-top condition, and some of the unusual jobs hand models do, from doubling for A-list celebrities one day to holding a piece of fruit for hours the next(n)

programme.title: The Sex Business
programme.desc: A disparate group of young sex workers reveal what life is really like trying to make a living as escorts, cammers and sex entrepreneurs. programme.desc: Michael Buerk, Shaun Williamson, Amanda Barrie, Anita Harris and John Prescott trade the countryside for cityscapes, drifting toward the industrial heart of the potteries in Stoke-on-Trent. As well as learning about the area's rich history along the way, they share some revelations of their own - such as who has showered the least, how to eat mushy peas without cutlery, and why ducks are scared of journalists. It's not all plain sailing, however, and after spending a rough few nights sleeping in the kitchen, John abandons ship to find an alternative(n)

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off

programme.title: Four in a Bed

The next morning, the DIY breakfast proves underwhelming for some, while the hosts discover that the great outdoors is not everyone's cup of tea(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Former hotel inspector and owner David Stanbridge and manager Nikki Deans welcome the contestants to the Cley Hall Hotel in Spalding, Lincolnshire. Peter and Amanda are impressed by their room, but Simon and Lyn are unhappy with the state of the carpet. The next morning, the guests decide to put their eight-minute breakfast rule to the test, and some of them have concerns about Nikki's waitressing skills(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The hosts meet for the final time to find out what they've been paid and confront their critics. Simon and Lyn kick off proceedings by demanding answers from John about his disturbed night's sleep, but then find themselves taken to task by David over their cleanliness feedback. Tensions run high as the payments are revealed and the winner is announced(n)

Only one can claim star baker, while one of the competitors will have to leave the tent for good(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien explore a fairground, and collect a bicycle made for two, a ship's search lamp and a lambing chair(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at stately home Anstey Hall in Cambridge, with father and son team John and Johnny de Bruyne. The fun starts with a character-revealing afternoon of goose-herding, before the participants dress to impress at a formal dinner. John recounts stories of royal connections - which fails to impress hotel managers Mark and Layla. In the morning, as they tuck in to a self-service breakfast, guest Tim decides that DIY doesn't work at Anstey Hall, but when he notes this on his feedback form, John and Johnny accuse him of game-playing(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the competition is to clothes-free Clover Spa and Hotel in Birmingham, where owner Tim Higgs hopes to convert his rivals to the joys of naturism. Judging with an extra critical eye are John and Jonny De Bruyne, who received negative feedback from Tim, and a thorough room inspection reveals a dusty porn DVD. Tim tries to encourage his nervous guests to take part in a naked spa experience, but John opts to watch television instead. When feedback is invited, Tim is delighted to discover Mark and Layla would stay again but shocked by the 'porn film' discovery(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the Angel Hotel in Coleford, Gloucestershire for the third day of the competition, where a treasure hunt organised by hosts Mark and Layla brings out their guests' competitive sides. Meanwhile, there is confusion at breakfast and Layla's manners are criticised(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final stay is at Higher Gitcombe in Totnes, Devon, where owners Paul and Lynnie Jolly set out to spoil their guests. Everyone is overwhelmed by the added extras, but naturist Tim is put off by cushions and clutter. After a masterclass in Nordic walking, Lynnie serves up a veg smoothie, which unhealthy eaters Mark and Layla dubiously down in one. Over dinner, Tim challenges his host's organic 'niche'. And Layla, still smarting from his negative feedback, labels him 'weird, naked Tim'. At breakfast, guests are wary as Tim's order goes wrong and Jonny and Layla engage in an eating competition. When feedback is invited, the Jollys are affronted by accusations of special treatment and vow to put the record straight(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: On the final day of the competition, the owners of Anstey Hall, the Clover Spa and Hotel, the Angel Hotel and Higher Gitcombe meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and settle some scores. Having aired their issues, the winners are announced(n)

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer come to the aid of the Oberoi family from Harrow, whose house has a fridge freezer and in the hallway and a single room for their two children. Kirstie comes up with a plan to expand the kitchen and make the lounge more welcoming, while Phil shows them some of the other homes on the market(n)

programme.title: Love It or List It Australia
programme.desc: Andrew Winter and Neale Whitaker help Brendan and James choose between moving and renovating their cottage in the Melbourne inner-city suburb of Brunswick(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Eastern black rhino Zuri gives birth to baby Ike, and sun bears Toni and Millie are having relationship issues. Meanwhile, Bactrian camel Shan-shan is under the weather, so the Chester keepers are trying to work out how to help her(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick and his team treat Staffordshire bull terrier Lulu, who has been hit by a car and had her pelvis smashed into more than 25 pieces. The professor's only option is to pin the larger pieces back together in an operation that will require all his expertise. Noel and his team also treat a giant schnauzer called Florence who has a sore knee and a pointy-eared Jack Russell called Muffin who has a mysterious problem with her back legs(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree only have a few weeks to prepare for their wedding, but the chateau is nowhere near ready to receive the 200 guests they have invited and finding reliable, affordable workers is proving difficult. Dick oversees the damming of the moat and the installation of a new, eco-friendly sewage system, while Angel turns her attention to giving the rooms a distinctive decor(n)

programme.title: Outlander
programme.desc: In 1968 Scotland, Claire, Brianna and Roger struggle to trace Jamie's whereabouts in history, leaving Claire to wonder if they will ever find him again(n)

programme.title: The Billion Pound Hotel
programme.desc: Shaped like a giant sail and standing more than 1,000ft tall, the Burj Al Arab in Dubai cost an estimated £1billion to build, and has been dubbed the world's most luxurious hotel. Guests paying anything up to £10,000 a night expect only the highest of standards, so what does it take for the staff to satisfy these demanding customers? This programme follows the stories of a variety of visitors, from the King of Uganda to a firefighter who has saved up months of wages, and the workers who serve them(n)

programme.title: It Was Alright In
programme.desc: Matt Lucas narrates another look back at TV shows from the 1970s, focusing on the comedies, documentaries and public information films that made people so anxious 40 years ago. Some of the famous shows covered include Man Alive and Brass Tacks, as well as The Goodies taking on the police and June Whitfield burning her bra. The show features first-hand accounts from the people who were making telly at the time, including Barry Cryer, Janet Street-Porter and Bill Oddie(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A man suffers serious spinal injuries after falling off a mountain bike. As doctors assess the extent of the damage, his wife recalls growing up in apartheid-era South Africa. A medical team gives emergency oxygen to an 83-year-old woman who is struggling to breathe, and a woman is rushed to A&E after her daughter accidently crashed into a lamp post(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick and his team treat Staffordshire bull terrier Lulu, who has been hit by a car and had her pelvis smashed into more than 25 pieces. The professor's only option is to pin the larger pieces back together in an operation that will require all his expertise. Noel and his team also treat a giant schnauzer called Florence who has a sore knee and a pointy-eared Jack Russell called Muffin who has a mysterious problem with her back legs(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Eastern black rhino Zuri gives birth to baby Ike, and sun bears Toni and Millie are having relationship issues. Meanwhile, Bactrian camel Shan-shan is under the weather, so the Chester keepers are trying to work out how to help her(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty investigates the popular belief that coffee can sober people up and also looks into new research linking consumption of the beverage and a reduced risk of liver disease. In San Francisco, Kate Quilton investigates trendy new drink kombucha, while Matt Tebbutt examines why supermarket labels don't say which cut of beef thin-cut steaks come from(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Fill Your House for Free
programme.desc: Kirstie helps young couple Ferne and Peter, transform their empty flat into a brilliant space for entertaining, with the help of some crafty upcycling(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty sets out to learn how kippers are produced, and finds out why this nutritious smoked fish is no longer a breakfast favourite. Kate Quilton is in Germany to investigate gherkins, discovering a space-age machine that can pickle food in seconds. Matt Tebbutt and Helen Lawal try to discover why rice cakes are white when they are made of brown rice(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick and Angel expand the project by setting up a luxury campsite on the chateau grounds, but they only have six weeks to complete it before the first guests arrive - and the weather is not on their side, with constant rain turning the planned site into a muddy field. They also have to contend with a visit from Dick's family at Easter(n)

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It's Dessert Week and judges Prue Leith and Paul Hollywood set three sticky challenges to test the remaining nine bakers. There is a meringue Signature, a wobbly Technical, and a complex chocolate Showstopper. Which bakers will impress Prue and Paul enough to keep their place in the tent? Noel Fielding and Sandi Toksvig announce who will be staying and leaving at the end of the show(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit a landed gent in Gloucestershire, converting a gilded picture frame into a flat-screen TV surround and hoping to sell a Fordson tractor(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Four sets of campsite proprietors battle it out to be named best value, with the competition beginning at Amber's Bell Tent Camping in Mannington, Norfolk(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second day of the competition is at Park Farm Camping and Caravan Site in Norfolk, where husband and wife team Derek and Julie Nice are proud to show off their budget, family-friendly site. Former marksman Derek treats his guests to a spot of clay pigeon shooting, bringing out his competitive side(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to 500-year-old Layer Marney Tower in Essex for the third day of the competition, where they are welcomed into luxury glamping lodges by Nick and Sheila Charrington. The guests have little to complain about with the accommodation, but a revelation over dinner changes how they see things. Plus, a treasure hunt organised by the hosts sends one team on a wild goose chase(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Day four is at the Foxhunter Park in Kent, where brother and sister Jane Stacey and Russell Saunders own a caravan site that includes a bistro and entertainment. The accommodation is luxurious but the large scale of the site does not sit well with the campers - and the evening entertainment of a fire-eating limbo dancer leaves most of them cold(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The campers meet for the final time to find out what they thought one another's stay was worth. Amber is offended by Derek's low scoring, while he is thrown by Nick and Sheila's feedback. Russell and Jane have an axe to grind with Derek whose low scoring of their park is made worse by what they see as a personal attack on their hosting, while Nick and Sheila appear to emerge relatively unscathed(n)

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: In Brixworth, Northamptonshire, rugby fan Ian wants to leave the property that he and wife Sarah bought together two years ago. With a toddler and a couple of dogs, their two-bed rural semi is bursting at the seams. However, Sarah believes the house has the potential to cater for a growing family and wants to stay - if they can sort out their dingy ground floor. Kirstie wants to open up the downstairs and drive a digger through the conservatory, but will Ian and Sarah like the idea?(n)

programme.title: Love It or List It Australia
programme.desc: Andrew Winter and Neale Whitaker go head-to-head to work out the best solution for homeowners to either renovate their properties or relocate. Chris and Jess live in a classic but dysfunctional Queenslander house with their four children in the Coorparoo suburb of Brisbane. But while Jess wants to stay, Chris thinks it's time to upgrade to something bigger(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: It's nightfall at Chester and the zoo's 800 nocturnal inhabitants start to wake up. Aardvark Koos is showing a potentially romantic interest in his sister Himba, but the zoo needs to ensure that their animals are breeding from the right genetic pool. It's also time for mouse-deer Una to leave her parents and meet a mate(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Basset hound Barney is 'top dog' at home, enjoying salmon and venison as some of his favourite treats. But lately Barney's vertebrae have been causing him problems and Noel Fitzpatrick needs to perform high-risk surgery using 3D-printed implants. The professor also treats a giant mastiff called Tank, who is suffering from severe arthritis and needs radical surgery on both elbows, and a family puppy who has fractured his foot jumping off a bed. Now he requires specially developed surgical treatment(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree only have a few weeks to prepare for their wedding, but the chateau is nowhere near ready to receive the 200 guests they have invited and finding reliable, affordable workers is proving difficult. Dick oversees the damming of the moat and the installation of a new, eco-friendly sewage system, while Angel turns her attention to giving the rooms a distinctive decor(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Moped delivery man Hussnain, 24, is rushed to A&E after being caught up in an accident involving a police car chasing another vehicle. Seventy-six-year-old Edward is brought in due to his breathing difficulties and a serious pre-existing heart condition, while part-time wrestlers Kieran and James come in after Kieran injures his knee in a bout(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: The return of the programme following the emergency services, this time focusing on the work of the police, paramedics and fire service in Wiltshire. The first edition examines the rise in racially aggravated hate crimes, with the mother of a nine-year-old girl calling 999 to report that her daughter has been racially abused by two 11-year-olds while out playing. PC James Isaac arrests a 29-year-old man who targeted a 17-year-old white girl with text messages after coming across photos of her and her black boyfriend on Facebook(n)

programme.desc: Racing instructor Aktar has a garage full of cars, and is going to sell them all to buy his 'forever car'. Will it be the American muscle, the German turbos or an English supercar?(n)

programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May each buy a rear-wheel-drive coupe for £1,500 and find themselves entered into a French ice race. programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson gets behind the wheel of the Ferrari F12 Berlinetta, sampling the punch of its 730bhp V12 engine on the open roads of Scotland. programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The three motoring enthusiasts head to Spain on a mission to help its struggling economy - but the convertible supercars they take are very expensive and anything but discreet. With Jeremy Clarkson in a McLaren MP4-12C, Richard Hammond driving a Ferrari 458 and James May behind the wheel of an Audi R8 V10, the trio encounter mountain passes, empty towns and an abandoned airport before creating their own street circuit in a deserted Madrid development. Plus, Sherlock star Benedict Cumberbatch sets a lap time in the Reasonably Priced Car(n)

programme.title: Walter Tull - Forgotten Hero
programme.desc: Former EastEnders actor Nick Bailey investigates the story of Walter Tull, the pioneering black British footballer who died heroically fighting in World War One. He examines how Walter became the first black officer in the British Army when regulations required men of pure European descent, asks why he was denied the honour of a Military Cross for heroism and explores how he overcame racial abuse(n)

programme.title: Catching History's Criminals: The Forensics Story
programme.desc: Gabriel Weston explores the forensic advances that have elevated murder weapons from mere instruments to crucial pieces of evidence. She sees how arsenic, the undetectable weapon of choice in the 19th century, was exposed as the killer thanks to one simple chemical test, and finds out how distinctive marks on a victim's skull led to the weapon and, in turn, the killer. Gabriel also looks at the latest forensic developments, from 3-D laser scanning that can reconstruct the sequence of events at the scene of a gun crime to the pioneering chemistry able to determine where in the world someone has been based on just a few strands of their hair(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how recent archaeological discoveries have shed new light on culture, origins and capabilities of the very first native Britons. For centuries, it has been assumed Britain's first peoples lives a brutal, hand-to-mouth existence as hunter-gatherers, but fresh evidence has emerged that has forced scientists and anthropologist to reassess their impressions of who these people were. The result is a very different portrait, illustrating a hardy and sophisticated culture that withstood centuries of extreme climate change, including a devastating tsunami, and narrator Adrian Lester describes how these early Britons endured the primal forces and became the first people to populate the island nation that would eventually become known as Britain. Includes contributions by a variety of experts, including Dr Hans Peeters, Dr Jim Innes, Professor Vince Gaffney, Dr David Jacques and Professor Martin Bell(n)

programme.desc: Part one of two. Biologist Patrick Aryee explores how cats have evolved throughout the millennia, learning to thrive in inhospitable environments, as well as becoming the world's most popular pet. In the first edition there is spectacular footage of caracals jumping three metres to catch flying birds, and a rare Bornean bay cat, which was thought to have been extinct. Patrick finds out how tigers made the transition from South-east Asia's steamy jungles to the -40C temperatures of Siberia, and meets 'lion whisperer' Kevin Richardson(n)

programme.desc: Every 12 hours a child in the UK is taken by a parent or family member, with the guardian left behind often going through years of heartache and red tape just to see them again. This documentary follows three parents on emotional and dramatic journeys as they fight their ex-partners through the international courts in a bid to get their children back from foreign countries.
